response.justification: Bogdan has tangential relevance through his GISCollective geo-data platform and early C/C++ work at National Instruments, but his core expertise is firmly in TypeScript/Node.js web development. The C++ experience is sparse, dated (2007–2008), and limited to IDE tooling rather than systems or geospatial pipelines. There is no Rust experience, no routing or navigation pipeline work, and no evidence of the low-level systems knowledge (fault tolerance, consistency, service discovery in a geospatial context) this role demands. His high-throughput distributed systems work at Sociomantic is interesting but is over a decade old and unrelated to routing data.
strengths: GISCollective demonstrates genuine, long-running commitment to geospatial data platforms with real-world deployment
Proven experience building high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic using async I/O and high-performance databases)
Broad systems exposure across embedded, mobile, desktop, and server environments shows adaptability and technical range
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over 15 years old; Rust is entirely absent from the resume and skills list
No experience with routing tiles, navigation guidance systems, or map data transformation pipelines
Primary technology stack (TypeScript, Node.js, MongoDB) is orthogonal to the C++/Linux systems engineering this role requires

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation pipeline experience
